data.description:
## Why

The `rebar.*` library facade returns plain `dict`/`list` shapes with no named,
documented return contract, so published-library consumers cannot tell which keys
are guaranteed or get type-checker support. The review flagged this as the main
library-API gap. rebar's public contract is *already* the canonical JSON Schemas
in `schemas/`, and it already has a derived-artifact + CI drift-gate pattern
(prompt-index gate, criteria-routing parity gate) to keep derived artifacts in
sync. Pydantic is deliberately NOT a core dependency (core is
`pyyaml`/`jsonschema`/`referencing`), so runtime models are off the table for the
base facade.

## What

Give the full `rebar.*` facade a typed return contract **additively and
statically**, via schema-derived `TypedDict`s:

- Generate `TypedDict` definitions for the public return shapes **from the
  canonical JSON Schemas** (single source of truth), covering the full facade:
  create/claim/transition results, ticket state, gate results, links,
  file-impact, verify-commands, session-logs, workflow runs, search.
- Annotate the `rebar.*` function return types with these TypedDicts. **Zero
  runtime change** — returns stay plain dicts; existing callers are untouched.
- Add a **CI drift-gate** (same style as the prompt-index gate) that regenerates
  the TypedDicts from the schemas and fails if the committed types are stale.
- Document the stable exception surface: `RebarError` (base) and
  `ConcurrencyError` are already defined/exported in `rebar._errors` — document
  them (and any others promoted) as the stable, public exception contract.

## Scope

Schema-derived TypedDicts + a generator + a CI drift-gate + facade type
annotations + exception docs. Static-typing only: NO Pydantic/dataclass runtime
models, NO new core runtime dependency, NO change to any return value. Pairs with
the API-stability matrix story (the Python-facade row).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Public `rebar.*` return shapes have `TypedDict` types generated from the
  canonical JSON Schemas, covering the full facade.
- [ ] Facade functions are annotated with these types; `mypy`/`pyright` over
  `src/rebar` still passes; no runtime return value changes.
- [ ] A CI drift-gate regenerates the TypedDicts from schemas and fails on stale
  committed types (mirroring the prompt-index gate).
- [ ] `RebarError`/`ConcurrencyError` (and any promoted siblings) are documented
  as the stable public exception surface.
- [ ] No new core runtime dependency is introduced; `make lint` + type checks
  stay green.
data.title: Typed public library models: schema-derived TypedDicts for the rebar.* facade, drift-gated
